title: kubectl
description: Command-line tool for communicating with a Kubernetes cluster API server.

entries.label: List pods
entries.description: Lists all pods across all namespaces. Drop -A for current namespace only.

entries.label: Apply a manifest
entries.description: Creates or updates resources defined in a YAML manifest. Use -k for kustomize.

entries.label: Stream pod logs
entries.description: Streams logs from a pod. Use -f to follow, -c to pick a specific container.

entries.label: Describe a resource
entries.description: Shows detailed info including events, limits, and status for troubleshooting.

entries.label: Exec into a pod
entries.description: Opens an interactive shell inside a running pod for debugging.

entries.label: Port forward to local
entries.description: Forwards a local port to a service/pod port. Useful for local debugging.

entries.label: Rollout restart deployment
entries.description: Triggers a rolling restart of all pods in a deployment with zero downtime.

entries.label: Scale a deployment
entries.description: Changes the number of running pod replicas for a deployment.

entries.label: Resource usage metrics
entries.description: Shows CPU and memory usage per pod. Requires metrics-server to be installed.

entries.label: Switch context
entries.description: Switches the active kubeconfig context to target a different cluster.
